description_long: This conference center is a large, circular building with a distinctive thatched roof and a central open courtyard. The exterior walls are constructed with a visible timber frame structure, filled with lighter panels, and feature multiple evenly spaced vertical supports. The roof is predominantly made of straw or thatch, sloping gently towards the center, where a raised, polygonal section with a flagpole sits above the main entrance. The building has three prominent protruding sections, likely serving as main entrances or lobbies, each capped with its own gabled roof. The overall design is reminiscent of historical Elizabethan theaters, with a rustic yet monumental appearance. The structure appears to be multi-leveled, with open galleries visible through the upper sections of the facade.
description: A large, circular conference center with a thatched roof, timber-framed exterior, and three prominent gabled entrance sections. The design is reminiscent of a historical theater with open galleries and a central courtyard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a gabled roof, central flagpole, and visible timber framing.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the thatched roof's slope and one of the protruding entrance sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous circular facade, central roof structure, and absence of main entrances.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing another entrance section and the open courtyard within the roof.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance is located at the front, marked by a large, protruding gabled section with a thatched roof matching the main structure. The entrance appears to have double doors, possibly wooden, set within a timber-framed facade. There are no visible steps or ramps, but the entrance is distinguished by its size, central placement, and the architectural emphasis of the gable and framing.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a continuous row along the upper section of the circular facade, appearing as evenly spaced rectangular openings between timber supports. The windows are uniform in size and shape, forming a rhythmic pattern around the building. There are no visible decorative details or significant variation in window design.
attribute_roof_structure: The roof is a large, circular, thatched structure with a gentle slope towards the center, culminating in a raised polygonal section topped with a flagpole. The roof overhangs the facade slightly and is constructed from thick layers of straw or thatch. The three gabled entrance sections each have their own smaller thatched roofs, integrated seamlessly with the main roof.
attribute_architectural_style: The building exhibits a historical, Elizabethan architectural style, characterized by its circular form, timber framing, thatched roofing, and open galleries. The design is reminiscent of iconic structures like the Globe Theatre, with rustic materials and minimal ornamentation, emphasizing function and communal gathering.
